status: DEV — clinician_review pending; NOT patient-facing; reference-only (engine-isolated). Therapeutic drug monitoring ranges/timing (APF22 Table B.2 facts). Target CONCENTRATIONS, not doses; NTI is the bucket under the TDM heading. Records author to review_status 'draft'; require clinician verification against source + AMH/TGA.

attestation.statement: tdm_parameters reference register — APF22-cited facts / public primary sources; NOT prescribing, NOT a dose source.

records.monitoring_indication: efficacy and nephrotoxicity avoidance in serious infection
records.notes: AUC-guided dosing increasingly preferred over trough alone

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2

records.active_metabolite_note: highly protein bound (~90%); interpret against albumin — free level may be needed if albumin low

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2

records.monitoring_indication: trough kept low to limit nephro/ototoxicity; baseline renal function required

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2

records.monitoring_indication: poor concentration-effect correlation; routine monitoring not generally recommended
records.notes: 350-700 micromol/L; up to 150 mg/L tolerated in some patients; monitoring is most useful to confirm toxicity, assess adherence or interpret interactions

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

records.notes: adult range; child range approx 10-30 mg/L; long half-life (approx 15-25 days) so slow to reach steady state

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

records.monitoring_indication: adherence and dose interpretation, especially around pregnancy/OCP
records.notes: 12-56 micromol/L; clearance rises in pregnancy and with the combined oral contraceptive; valproate co-administration roughly doubles the half-life while enzyme inducers shorten it

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

records.notes: adult range; steady-state peak sampled ~2-4 h post-dose; clearance reduced by heart failure, liver disease and some drugs (carbamazepine, cimetidine, ciprofloxacin, erythromycin) and increased by smoking

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

records.monitoring_indication: non-linear kinetics; toxicity risk, notably in poor CYP2D6 metabolisers
records.notes: 0.5-2.0 micromol/L; non-responders target 0.60-1.2 mg/L; poor metabolisers have longer half-lives and greater toxicity risk

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

records.monitoring_indication: confirm adequacy vs toxicity; especially in renal impairment
records.notes: sample pre-dose or >6 h post-dose; lower targets apply in heart failure (approx 0.6-1.0 nanomol/L); toxicity more likely with hypokalaemia/hypercalcaemia even within range; end-stage renal failure prolongs time to steady state

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

records.monitoring_indication: narrow index; prophylaxis vs acute mania targets differ
records.notes: standard sample is 12 h post-dose; acute-mania target approx 0.8-1.2 mmol/L; toxic threshold lower in the elderly (>1.2 mmol/L); levels rise with renal impairment, diuretics, low-sodium diet, pregnancy and infection

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

records.notes: 17-50 micromol/L in SI units; autoinduces its own metabolism; pre-dose timing less critical with slow-release formulations. NB the nti register lists 4-10 mg/L for this drug — reconcile source

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section B Table B.2 'Drugs commonly monitored in clinical practice'
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; concentration targets, NOT doses)

record.monitoring_indication: sample at least 6 hours post-dose (distribution); lower targets in heart failure

record.monitoring_indication: narrow therapeutic index; sample 12 hours post-dose
